questions.question: Describe the friendship between Sue and Johnsy.
questions.answer: Sue and Johnsy shared a strong bond of friendship. They lived together, supported each other as artists, and Sue took great care of Johnsy when she became ill.

questions.question: Explain Johnsy’s strange belief about the ivy leaves.
questions.answer: Johnsy believed that her life was connected to the ivy leaves outside her window. She thought she would die when the last leaf fell from the creeper.

questions.question: How did Sue try to distract Johnsy from her illness?
questions.answer: Sue tried to distract Johnsy by painting in her room, talking cheerfully, and encouraging her to focus on other things instead of the falling leaves.

questions.question: Describe the role of the doctor in the story.
questions.answer: The doctor examined Johnsy and told Sue that medicine alone could not cure her. He believed that Johnsy needed the will to live in order to recover.

questions.question: Who was Behrman and what kind of person was he?
questions.answer: Behrman was an old painter who lived in the same building as Sue and Johnsy. He was kind-hearted and cared deeply for the young artists.

questions.question: What was Behrman’s lifelong dream?
questions.answer: Behrman’s lifelong dream was to paint a masterpiece, but he had never been able to achieve it.

questions.question: Describe the stormy night in the story.
questions.answer: During the stormy night, heavy rain and strong winds blew outside. Despite the harsh weather, the last leaf appeared to remain on the creeper.

questions.question: What surprised Johnsy the next morning?
questions.answer: Johnsy was surprised to see that the last leaf had not fallen despite the storm.

questions.question: Explain how Johnsy’s attitude changed during the story.
questions.answer: At first, Johnsy had lost hope and wanted to die. Later, after seeing the leaf remain, she regained hope and decided to live.

questions.question: How did Sue take care of Johnsy during her illness?
questions.answer: Sue stayed with Johnsy, cooked soup for her, encouraged her, and tried to keep her hopeful throughout her illness.

questions.question: Describe the discovery made after Behrman’s death.
questions.answer: After Behrman’s death, a ladder, lantern, and paints were found in his room, revealing that he had painted the last leaf during the storm.

questions.question: Why did Behrman paint the leaf on the wall?
questions.answer: Behrman painted the leaf to give Johnsy hope and save her life.

questions.question: Explain the significance of the last leaf.
questions.answer: The last leaf symbolizes hope and the will to live. It changes Johnsy’s mindset and inspires her to fight her illness.

questions.question: Why is Behrman’s painting called a masterpiece?
questions.answer: Behrman’s painting is called a masterpiece because it saved Johnsy’s life and gave her hope.

questions.question: Describe the importance of hope in the story.
questions.answer: Hope plays a central role in the story. When Johnsy regains hope, she begins to recover from her illness.

questions.question: What lesson does Johnsy learn from the last leaf?
questions.answer: Johnsy learns that it is wrong to give up hope and that life is precious.

questions.question: Explain the theme of sacrifice in the story.
questions.answer: Behrman sacrifices his life to paint the leaf and save Johnsy. His act shows selfless love and kindness.

questions.question: How does the story show the importance of friendship?
questions.answer: Sue’s dedication and care for Johnsy show the strength of true friendship.

questions.question: What role does the ivy creeper play in the story?
questions.answer: The ivy creeper becomes the focus of Johnsy’s belief and symbolizes her hope and recovery.

questions.question: Describe Johnsy’s recovery in the story.
questions.answer: Johnsy gradually regains hope and begins to take interest in life again after seeing the last leaf.

questions.question: Explain the importance of the storm in the story.
questions.answer: The storm creates tension and highlights the bravery of Behrman when he goes out to paint the leaf.

questions.question: What does Behrman’s character represent?
questions.answer: Behrman represents kindness, sacrifice, and the true spirit of an artist.

questions.question: Why is the story emotionally powerful?
questions.answer: The story is emotionally powerful because of Behrman’s sacrifice and Johnsy’s recovery.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of the title 'The Last Leaf'.
questions.answer: The title refers to the leaf that symbolizes hope and becomes the turning point in Johnsy’s life.

questions.question: Summarise the central message of the story.
questions.answer: The story teaches that hope, friendship, and selfless sacrifice can change lives.
